# Copilot Coding Agent Onboarding — android-design-system

## Overview
Infomaniak Android Design System — a Compose-based UI library providing design tokens and per-product themes. Tokens are generated from Figma via JSON and a Kotlin script. Published modules: `Foundation` (core tokens + `EsdsTheme`) and per-product `Theme*` modules.

## Build
```bash
./gradlew build   # Foundation, PrimitiveTokens, app (demo), catalog
```

## Token Update Workflow
1. Modify `token-source/android_specific_tokens.json`
2. CI (`.github/workflows/generate-android-specific-tokens.yml`) auto-generates code and pushes to the PR branch — **do not manually edit generated token files**
3. After CI push, manually create/update the public `EsdsTheme.Values` instance in each `Theme*` module

## Project Layout
```
Foundation/             # Core theme infrastructure — EsdsTheme, token interfaces
PrimitiveTokens/        # Raw primitive token values (auto-generated — do not edit)
app/                    # Demo/sample app
catalog/                # Compose component catalog
build-logic/            # Convention plugins
scripts/generateAndroidSpecificTokens.main.kts   # Token generation script
token-source/android_specific_tokens.json        # Source of truth — edit this, not generated files
```

## PR Review Instructions

- **Never manually edit files in `PrimitiveTokens/`** — they are auto-generated by the CI script triggered by changes to `token-source/android_specific_tokens.json`.
- After token generation, each `Theme*` module needs a public `EsdsTheme.Values` instance for each dimension: Light, LightMediumContrast, LightHighContrast, Dark, DarkMediumContrast, DarkHighContrast.
- Use only Material3 Compose components (`androidx.compose.material3`).
- When adding/removing a runtime dependency, update `LICENSES.md` at the repo root.
